title: Code: The Hidden Language of Computer Hardware and Software
author: Charles Petzold
summary: A beautifully written journey through the fundamentals of computing, from switches and circuits to assembly language and operating systems.
verdict: This book deepened my understanding of the invisible machinery under every program and every piece of hardware.

details.whyRead: I wanted to see the connections between physical circuits and the software abstractions I use every day.
details.expectation: I expected a gentle introduction to computers without deep engineering detail.
details.whatFound: Instead, I found clear engineering intuition built on physical components, binary logic, and elegant examples.
keyTakeaways: Computers are built from simple physical components.
High-level software is only meaningful when mapped to hardware behavior.
Understanding abstractions starts with understanding their physical roots.
details.whatChanged: It made me a better engineer by reminding me that every abstraction is built on something concrete.
details.favoriteQuote: A computer is made of silicon, but programming is made of ideas.
details.recommendation: Great for anyone who wants a richer sense of how hardware and software connect.
